body: {"rationale":"All 8 rubric criteria satisfied. (1) Title is imperative and names both subsystem (work/agent) and the change (serialize readiness before claim races). (2) Description has explicit PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE WITH five file:line anchors in execute_bead_loop.go, preclaim_intake_rewrite.go, and store.go, PROPOSED FIX with two concrete design alternatives, and NON-SCOPE. (3) Nine numbered AC items, each verifiable; AC 1–5 name specific Test* functions, AC 7 supplies a full `go test -run` filter covering all new and existing tests. (4) Wired-in assertion present at AC 6 (production ExecuteBeadWorker.Run must use the new boundary). (5) Final two AC lines (8, 9) are exactly `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... passes` and `lefthook run pre-commit passes`. (6) Labels carry phase:2, area:agent, area:beads, kind:bug, spec:FEAT-010, adr:023 — all required categories plus cross-refs. (7) Parent ddx-a9d130d0 stated; deps explicitly declared as none. (8) No /tmp or chat-session references anywhere; description is fully self-contained and gives an agent clear file targets and test names without operator hand-curation.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}

pre-close review: review-error: unparseable
Store.Claim moved before PreClaimIntakeHook/PreDispatchLintHook so the claim itself reserves the bead. Concurrent workers lose at Claim and skip model-backed intake/lint entirely. Terminal intake outcomes park (needs_human + intake.blocked event) and unclaim; lint-blocked unclaims. All six AC test names exist with assertions matching their stated guarantees.

{"rationale":"Strong bead overall. Title is imperative and names subsystem+change. Description has PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE with file:line citations, PROPOSED FIX with two alternative designs, NON-SCOPE, and DEPS sections. AC has 9 numbered criteria, at least 3 name specific Test* functions, AC7 is a `go test` filter covering new tests, AC8 is a full package run, AC9 is a lefthook gate, and AC6 explicitly names the WIRED-IN production boundary. Labels include phase, area, kind, reliability, spec, and adr cross-refs. Parent is set. Deps are stated explicitly. One minor gap: AC1-5 are aspirational test names that do not yet exist — the bead is filed before implementation, which is acceptable for a bug bead — but the description's PROPOSED FIX section presents two mutually exclusive design paths without indicating which is preferred, which could cause an autonomous agent to make a structural design choice without operator guidance. This ambiguity slightly increases risk of a wrong-direction execution but does not disqualify the bead.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":["In PROPOSED FIX, nominate a preferred design path (e.g., 'Preferred: claim-first with release on retryable errors') so an autonomous agent does not have to choose between the two designs.","Consider adding a concrete file to edit as the starting point (e.g., 'Start in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:686') to meet the self-test criterion fully."],"waivers_applied":[]}

The agent spent $1.01 and 711s but the error is pre-execution setup, not implementation. A prior attempt (20260507T063118-85f58780) succeeded at implementation (result_rev=95c2e54) but was blocked by an unparseable review verdict (review_malfunction). The bead itself is implementable and the previous attempt produced a mergeable result.","recommended_action":"retry","result_rev":"7c681820bd48192f64f90dd7bb42aab07e85f527","session_id":"eb-200d212a","status":"execution_failed","suggested_amendments":"none","suggested_followup_beads":[]}

{"rationale":"Strong bead overall. Title is imperative and names subsystem+change. Description has PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE (5 precise citations), PROPOSED FIX with 3 alternative designs spelled out, NON-SCOPE, and DEPS. AC has 9 numbered criteria, all specific and verifiable. Labels include phase, area, kind, spec, and adr cross-refs. Parent is set. Deps are explicit ('No deps'). Two deductions: (1) AC items 1-4 name new test functions that don't yet exist — no existing test or `go test -run` filter is cited as a baseline gate for the new work, which makes the wired-in assertion (AC6) the only production hook; this is acceptable but the bead would benefit from a 'red first' note confirming tests are expected to be written as part of this bead. (2) AC7's `-run` filter uses a partial prefix match (`TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaim|...`) which will work but the filter string is long and could silently miss a renamed test — minor robustness issue. Both are minor. The bead is self-contained, a cold agent could pick a file to edit and run tests without asking.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":["AC: Add a note on AC1-4 clarifying these are new tests to be authored as part of this bead (e.g., 'author and pass' rather than just 'passes'), so the executing agent knows to write them rather than search for them.","AC7: Consider splitting the long -run filter into two separate go test invocations (new tests, existing regression tests) so each is readable and a rename doesn't silently drop coverage."],"waivers_applied":[]}

rationale: All acceptance criteria for ddx-36eb046c are already satisfied in the current codebase.

The fix was committed in 9a3ad75a5 ("fix(work): serialize model-backed readiness behind atomic claim [ddx-36eb046c]") prior to this execution worktree's base rev 501791cb0.

Evidence:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: Store.Claim at line 687 precedes PreClaimIntakeHook at line 722 (AC #6 wired-in)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_preclaim_serial_test.go: all 4 AC tests present
  - TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaimIntakeRunsOncePerBead (AC #1)
  - TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreDispatchLintDoesNotDuplicateEvents (AC #2)
  - TestPreClaimIntakeRewriteRequiresOwnedReservation (AC #3)
  - TestExecuteBeadWorkerReadinessRejectReleasesOrParksClaim (AC #4)
- TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentWorkersDoNotDoubleExecuteSameBead and TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s still pass (AC #5)
